component.name: SynchroSonic
component.summary: Linux-first LAN audio casting and receiver control

description.p: Metadata and release consistency update for the SynchroSonic desktop app.
This release aligns the workspace, app, packaging, documentation, and release automation on version 0.1.7 and the canonical AudioShare repository owned by UdayaSri0.

SynchroSonic is a Linux-first GTK4/libadwaita desktop application for capturing system audio, discovering receivers, casting over the local network, running a receiver mode, and inspecting stream, sync, and buffer health.

The current release track focuses on Linux PipeWire tooling, LAN transport, and explicit diagnostics. Bluetooth support is intentionally limited to selecting local Linux playback outputs exposed by PipeWire.
The current public release line uses version 0.1.7 and the canonical AudioShare GitHub repository for source, releases, and issue tracking.
